channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High Rates of Homelessness Persist Despite Interim Shelter Programs in L.A. County
item.description: Los Angeles County's interim housing programs, designed to temporarily shelter and stabilize people experiencing homelessness, are struggling to prevent residents from returning to the streets. Accord...

item.title: Single winner claims $15 million in Oz Lotto draw #1678
item.description: On Tuesday evening, one extremely lucky Australian punter won the entire $15 million jackpot in Oz Lotto draw #1678. The identity and state or territory of the winner have not yet been disclosed, leav...

item.title: Mark Carney wins Canadian majority, strengthening Liberals’ power until 2029
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has achieved a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government following a series of special elections in Ontario and Quebec. The pivotal victory in University–Ro...

item.title: Kenya Starts Nairobi Green Cities Project with UN Support to Improve Climate Resilience
item.description: The Government of Kenya, together with the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) and UN-Habitat, has rolled out a Sh520 million initiative to turn Nairobi into a model for low-carbon, climate-re...

item.title: Insurers urge Carney to prioritise climate action amid rising wildfire threats
item.description: As Canada enters a new wildfire season, the nation’s insurers are taking proactive measures to reinforce homes against growing climate-related threats. Insurance providers have begun working with loca...

item.title: NACADA Enhances Probation Officers' Skills to Combat Substance Abuse in Nairobi
item.description: Nairobi — The National Authority for the Campaign Against Alcohol and Drug Abuse (NACADA) has taken proactive steps to address the rising challenge of substance abuse in Kenya by training 66 probation...

item.title: Structural risks behind Africa’s public sector expansion in 2026
item.description: In 2026, Africa public sector dey experience strong expansion as many governments across the continent dey increase spending on infrastructure, transport systems, energy projects, and digital governan...

item.title: Hungary’s incoming prime minister signals intent to urge Putin to end Ukraine conflict
item.description: Following his decisive election victory over Viktor Orban, Péter Magyar, the incoming prime minister of Hungary, has indicated he would seek to persuade Vladimir Putin to cease hostilities in Ukraine....

item.title: Tanzania-Comoros Trade Imbalance Creates Opportunities for Exporters
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The trade balance between Tanzania and Comoros is showing a huge difference, which experts now see as an opportunity for new business ventures and market growth. Between 2023 and 2025,...

item.title: Fire Destroys Several Shops at Kasauli Heritage Market Including Iconic Bun-Samosa Shop
item.description: In the early hours of Monday, a fire broke out at the century-old Heritage Market in Kasauli, Solan district, Himachal Pradesh, causing significant damage to eight shops and estimated losses of around...

item.title: PARI to Launch Awit Museum Celebrating Filipino Music and Artists
item.description: The Philippines is set to welcome the Awit Museum, a new cultural institution dedicated to celebrating the rich history of Filipino music and artists. Spearheaded by the Philippine Association of the ...

item.title: US Former Envoys Say Conflict Strengthens Iran's Negotiating Position on Nuclear Program
item.description: Former U.S. envoys involved in negotiating the 2015 Iran nuclear deal have expressed concern that the recent U.S.-Israeli military action against Iran and Tehran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz have...

item.title: One Nation Gains Could Threaten Labor in Victoria's Western Suburbs
item.description: Labor in Victoria is facing mounting challenges as One Nation, led by Pauline Hanson, gains traction among disaffected voters. Recent polling indicates a significant decline in Labor's primary vote, d...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Shows Price Gains Despite Softer Trading Week
item.description: For the week ending April 10, 2026, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) saw a quieter trading session, with overall turnover dropping by 36.9 per cent to TZS 13.88 billion from TZS 21.99 billion th...

item.title: Deadline for Filing 2025 Income Tax Returns Extended to May 15, 2026
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has announced a one-month extension for filing 2025 income tax returns (ITRs), moving the deadline from April 15 to May 15, 2026. This measure aims to give Filipino taxp...

item.title: Honda unveils Super-One EV inspired by the classic City Turbo II
item.description: Honda has unveiled the Super-One, a retro-styled electric vehicle that draws inspiration from the 1980s City Turbo II kei car. Designed as Japan’s answer to the Renault Twingo, the Super-One combines ...

item.title: Trump Criticises Italian PM Meloni Over Stance on Iran Conflict
item.description: On 14th April 2026, US President Donald Trump publicly criticised Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, accusing her of lacking courage regarding the Iran war. In an interview with the Italian newspa...

item.title: Southport inquiry identifies systemic failures and threatens to name officials over preventable attacks
item.description: The families of three young girls killed at a Southport dance class in June 2024 have voiced outrage at systemic failures across multiple agencies, following the publication of the Phase One report of...

item.title: NYC Health Concerns, Local Obituaries, Activism, and Real Estate Updates in Upper West Side Bulletin
item.description: The West Side Rag’s Monday bulletin for April 13, 2026 compiles several key developments affecting New York City’s Upper West Side. The report opens with environmental and public health concerns, noti...

item.title: Tanzania Considers Using Academic Certificates as Collateral for Youth Loans
item.description: Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba announced in Parliament that Tanzania is planning a legal framework to allow graduates to use their academic certificates as collateral for loans. This idea comes as man...
